entities.name: Systemic Dominance as Exclusion Response
entities.context: Framework explaining how industry dominance emerges as 'scar tissue' from historical exclusion of groups from power centers

entities.name: Parallel Institutional Infrastructure
entities.context: Alternative institutions built by excluded groups when barred from existing power centers

entities.name: Forced Autonomy
entities.context: Condition where lack of alternatives drives concentrated effort and specialized knowledge within excluded groups

entities.name: Outsider Efficiency
entities.context: Competitive advantage arising from internal cohesion and survival-driven optimization in parallel systems

entities.name: High-Density Networks
entities.context: Concentrated knowledge and connection networks that form within excluded groups

entities.name: Scarcity of Opportunity
entities.context: Described as a powerful engine for infrastructure creation; exclusion as lead indicator for future institution-building

relationships.context: Parallel institutions are the mechanism through which excluded groups eventually achieve dominance
Forced autonomy creates the conditions for building parallel institutional infrastructure
Forced autonomy produces high-density networks and specialized knowledge
Outsider efficiency is a benefit that allows parallel systems to outcompete original exclusionary institutions
Scarcity of opportunity drives infrastructure creation by excluded groups
